title: SSE buffer can grow unbounded when upstream never emits newlines
description:
## Severity
Critical
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/pipeline.rs:516-543
## Violation
The SSE parsing loop in `stream_sse_response` accumulates upstream bytes into an unbounded `String` until a `\n` is found:

```rust
let mut buffer = String::new();
...
while let Some(chunk_result) = stream.next().await {
    ...
    buffer.push_str(&String::from_utf8_lossy(&chunk));
    while let Some(line_end) = buffer.find('\n') {
        ...
    }
}
```
A hostile or broken upstream that streams large payloads without `\n` (or a single very long SSE event) will inflate `buffer` until the process OOMs. There is no cap on a single line, no cap on the buffer, and the only timeout (`last_data_time.elapsed()`) is reset by every byte received, so a 1 MiB/s newline-free stream is welcomed. The `String::from_utf8_lossy().to_string()` allocation also forces a full UTF-8 validation+copy on every chunk.
## Mandated refactor
Introduce `MAX_SSE_LINE_BYTES` (e.g. 1 MiB) and `MAX_SSE_BUFFER_BYTES` constants; abort the stream and emit `AppEvent::ApiError` when either is exceeded. Switch to `BytesMut` with a SIMD `memchr` scan over raw bytes to avoid the per-chunk UTF-8 round-trip. Track total bytes received against an absolute ceiling separate from the inactivity timeout.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
